trap: Query runs without enabled check on optional userId
description: enabled: !!userId for dependent queries to prevent fetch with undefined

trap: Loader prefetch with inline ad-hoc key, no shared queryOptions for the consuming component to rehydrate from
description: Server-side prefetch must derive from the SAME shared queryOptions the client component reads (the serialization bridge), so loader-fetched data is reused on mount rather than refetched. NOTE: the full dehydrate(queryClient)/<HydrationBoundary> handoff is NOT testable in this trap -- it provides only a client-side App() root and a void-returning loader(), no SSR render boundary to host HydrationBoundary; demanding it would force inventing a multi-file SSR scaffold absent from the prompt and would collide with the loader-staleTime-coordination assertion governing the same loader() fragment. See skill section 'SSR with Dehydrate/Hydrate' for the complete server/client pattern.
Query key factory pattern for hierarchical invalidation
Optimistic update: cancel queries, snapshot previous, rollback on error
Set default staleTime at QueryClient level (e.g. 60s)

trap: new Date() in query key (not serializable)
description: Query keys must be JSON-serializable -- use date.toISOString()

trap: initialData for loading placeholder (persists to cache)
description: placeholderData for loading UX, initialData for server-provided data
Fresh QueryClient per test with retry: false, gcTime: 0
invalidateQueries only refetches active -- use refetchType: all for inactive
Loader prefetch: use ensureQueryData + staleTime >= navigation time
